weeks.title: Week 1 — Foundation & Setup
weeks.theme: Get operational, meet your immediate circle, and absorb context. No pressure to produce yet.
goals: Fully working laptop, accounts, and tool access
Know your team members and your onboarding buddy
Understand what your team does and why it matters

days.label: Day 1
days.activities: Complete IT setup: laptop enrollment, VPN, email, Slack/Teams, and password manager. Meet your manager to review your schedule and 30-60-90 plan. Complete day-one compliance training.

days.label: Day 2
days.activities: Set up direct deposit and begin benefits enrollment. Meet your onboarding buddy. Request access to the systems your role needs (submit tickets early — approvals take time). Read the employee handbook.

days.label: Day 3
days.activities: Attend your first team meeting as a listener. Read your team's charter, roadmap, and recent status updates. Start a personal onboarding notes document — capture every acronym, name, and system you encounter.

days.label: Day 4
days.activities: Hold your first 1:1 with your manager: clarify what to own vs. escalate, communication preferences, and what success looks like at 30/60/90 days. Set up a recurring 1:1 slot.

days.label: Day 5
days.activities: Complete remaining required training modules. Review your access requests and chase any that are stuck. Recap the week with your buddy: what surprised you, what is still unclear.

checklist.item: Laptop, VPN, email, and chat fully working
Recurring 1:1 with manager on the calendar

weeks.title: Week 2 — People & Process
weeks.theme: Expand your network beyond the team, learn how work actually flows, and go deeper on the tools and systems of your role.
goals: Meet 5-8 key stakeholders outside your immediate team
Understand the end-to-end workflow your team operates in
Navigate the core tools of your role confidently

days.label: Day 6-7
days.activities: Schedule 25-minute intro 1:1s with the stakeholders your manager identified. Ask each: what does your team do, how do we work together, and what makes collaboration easy or hard?

days.label: Day 8
days.activities: Shadow a teammate through a full task cycle — from ticket pickup to completion. Note every tool, approval, and handoff involved. This reveals the real process, not the documented one.

days.label: Day 9
days.activities: Deep-dive the role-specific systems: repositories, pipelines, HRIS, boards, or compliance platforms depending on your role. Follow the runbooks or SOPs and flag anything outdated.

days.label: Day 10
days.activities: Attend all recurring team ceremonies this week (standup, planning, refinement, or staff meetings). Begin contributing small observations or questions. Check in with your manager on ramp-up pace.

checklist.item: One full workflow shadowed end to end
Core role tools accessed and explored
At least one outdated doc or gap flagged
Benefits enrollment finished or on track

weeks.title: Week 3 — First Contributions
weeks.theme: Shift from absorbing to producing. Deliver something small but real, and validate your understanding against reality.
goals: Complete your first real task or deliverable
Confirm alignment on expectations at the ~15-20 day mark

days.label: Day 11-12
days.activities: Pick up your first starter task — a small, well-scoped item your manager or lead suggests. The goal is to exercise the full workflow (do the work, get it reviewed, ship it), not to impress with size.

days.label: Day 13
days.activities: Ask for feedback on your first deliverable explicitly: what would you have done differently? Update the onboarding documentation with everything you found missing or unclear — a high-value early contribution.

days.label: Day 14
days.activities: Hold a check-in with your manager: review your 30-60-90 goals against your first two weeks, surface anything about the role that differs from expectations, and agree on week 4+ priorities.

days.label: Day 15
days.activities: Close out the onboarding sprint: confirm all access is granted, all training is done, and your notes document is organized. Identify one process improvement or gap you can own as a stretch item in your first 60 days.

checklist.item: First task completed, reviewed, and delivered
Onboarding docs improved with your findings
15-20 day expectation check-in held with manager
All access and training fully complete
